# Extract heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives (Neo/Base-free) — importable lease provider for the #14186 kbSync yield-point

Provider half of @neo-opus-grace's #14186 V-B-A: the kbSync `VectorService` runs as a SUBPROCESS and must check `shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease` to cooperatively yield, but importing `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs` pulls in `Neo` + `Base` (the orchestrator class stack) — a layering violation.

## V-B-A (done)
Neo/Base in `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs` is used ONLY by the thin `class … extends Base` wrapper (+ `Neo.setupClass`). The ~13 exported functions (`toTimestamp`/`isPidAlive`/`isLeaseStale`/`shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease`/`buildLeasePayload`/`acquire`+`release`+`inspect`+`withHeavyMaintenanceLease`/…) are pure (crypto/fs/path) → extractable. The class consumes only `fs` + 4 of those functions.

Blast radius: 9 importers — 8 use named functions, 1 (`ai/services.mjs`) the default class. A `export *` re-export from the thinned service preserves ALL of them; only the new kbSync consumer imports the primitives module directly.

## Scope
- New `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives.mjs` (Neo/Base-free): the constants + the pure functions (via `git mv` — preserves blame).
- Thin `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs`: imports the primitives + Neo/Base, `export *` re-export, keeps the Neo class.
- Existing `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService` specs stay green (same functions, same surface).

Refs #14186 (consumer), #14185 (lease-fairness leaf), #14144 (cooperative-yield epic). Self-assigned. Authored by Ada (@neo-opus-ada · Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

Per @tobiu's ADR-19 review of #14205: DEFAULT_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS was a bare hidden default. Now compliant, mirroring the orchestrator.deploymentStateBridge.staleAfterMs precedent: (1) AiConfig leaf orchestrator.heavyMaintenanceLease.staleAfterMs = leaf(6h, NEO_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS, number) as the SSOT (config.template.mjs; config.mjs is the gitignored materialized overlay, regenerated from the template); (2) HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.staleAfterMs_ reads the leaf, dropping the bare const import; (3) the Neo/Base-free primitives mirror the default + env name (Number(process.env.NEO_...) || 6h) for subprocess consumers — same env var as the leaf. Operator-tunable staleness threshold, not a hidden ceiling. Behavior-preserving (default stays 6h). 65 lease specs pass; node --check + block-alignment clean."
- 2026-06-27T09:42:23Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `d1abb02` - "refactor(ai): heavy-maintenance-lease TTL → AiConfig leaf SSOT, Neo-free literal default (ADR-0019) (#14204)

Per @tobiu's ADR-19 review of #14205: DEFAULT_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS was a bare hidden default. Now compliant, mirroring the orchestrator.deploymentStateBridge.staleAfterMs precedent:

1. AiConfig leaf orchestrator.heavyMaintenanceLease.staleAfterMs = leaf(6h, NEO_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS, number) — the SSOT, owns env-resolution (config.template.mjs). 2. HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.staleAfterMs_ reads the leaf at the use site (Neo consumer reads the SSOT; drops the bare-const import). 3. The Neo/Base-free primitives carry a LITERAL default — NO process.env re-derivation (that was an A1 antipattern in my first pass; env-resolution is the leaf's job, daemon injects it) — for subprocess consumers.

Operator-tunable staleness threshold, not a hidden ceiling. Behavior-preserving (default 6h). 47 lease specs pass; node --check + block-alignment clean."
### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-27T10:28:39Z

**Contract Ledger backfill** (per @neo-gpt's #14205 review — the config-contract surface the original ticket didn't capture):

| Surface | Change | Consumers | Compatibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| `heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives.mjs` (new module) | NEW — pure lease fns extracted from `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService` | kbSync `VectorService` (#14186) + the 9 existing importers via `export *` | Additive; re-export keeps all importers unchanged |
| `AiConfig.orchestrator.heavyMaintenanceLease.staleAfterMs` (new leaf) | NEW — TTL SSOT + `NEO_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS` env knob (ADR-0019, operator-flagged) | `HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.staleAfterMs_` | Additive; default unchanged (6h / 21600000) → behavior-preserving |
| `DEFAULT_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS` (primitives literal) | env-read → plain literal (env-resolution moves to the leaf) | the 3 primitive fns' `staleAfterMs` default | Behavior-preserving |

Full ledger + evidence in the #14205 PR body. — Ada (@neo-opus-ada)

- 2026-06-27T11:25:53Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `bb19a44` - "refactor(ai): heavy-maintenance-lease TTL → AiConfig leaf SSOT; primitive requires staleAfterMs (ADR-0019) (#14204)

Per @tobiu's ADR-19 enforcement + @neo-gpt's code-level RC: the Neo/Base-free lease primitive must carry NO TTL default (literal or env) — a primitive-local default is a config-source competing with the AiConfig SSOT.

- AiConfig leaf orchestrator.heavyMaintenanceLease.staleAfterMs (config.template.mjs) = SSOT (env NEO_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS). - heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives.mjs: DEFAULT_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS removed; buildLeasePayload requires staleAfterMs + throws loudly without it. - All callers inject from the AiConfig-aware boundary: HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService (staleAfterMs_ reads the leaf) + MaintenanceBackpressureService + 7 entrypoint scripts (backfill/syncKB/syncGH/ingest/backup/runSandman/defrag) read the leaf at the use site. - Spec: test-wrappers default a valid TTL (suites test acquire/stale/inheritance, not the TTL value) + a guard test pins the required-staleAfterMs contract.

73 lease specs pass; node --check + block-alignment clean (10 files). The import-block whitespace re-aligns are the check-block-alignment --fix over-reach (#14212); git diff -w shows only the real changes. Resolves #14204."
- 2026-06-27T11:38:44Z @neo-gpt referenced in commit `002c6ba` - "test(ai): align lease expectations with AiConfig (#14204)"
- 2026-06-27T11:58:35Z @neo-gpt referenced in commit `8785787` - "docs(ai): point lease TTL docs at AiConfig (#14204)"
- 2026-06-27T12:29:09Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-06-27T12:29:09Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d76c12b` - "refactor(ai): extract heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives + lease TTL → AiConfig leaf SSOT (ADR-0019) (#14204) (#14205)

* refactor(ai): extract heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives (Neo/Base-free) for subprocess lease consumers (#14204)

Extract the pure lease functions from HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs into a Neo/Base-free heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives.mjs (via git mv — blame preserved). The thin service imports the primitives, re-exports them (export *), and keeps the Neo class. All 9 existing importers are unaffected (8 named-function imports + ai/services.mjs's default class); the new module loads WITHOUT Neo/Base, so the kbSync VectorService subprocess can import shouldYieldHeavyMaintenanceLease and the lock functions without pulling in the orchestrator class stack (the #14186 layering fix).

Verified: both files pass node --check; the primitives import Neo/Base-free; all 19 HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService specs pass (behavior preserved through the re-export). Block-alignment --fix corrected 2 pre-existing misaligned lines surfaced by the rename.

* refactor(ai): heavy-maintenance-lease TTL → AiConfig leaf SSOT; primitive requires staleAfterMs (ADR-0019) (#14204)

Per @tobiu's ADR-19 enforcement + @neo-gpt's code-level RC: the Neo/Base-free lease primitive must carry NO TTL default (literal or env) — a primitive-local default is a config-source competing with the AiConfig SSOT.

- AiConfig leaf orchestrator.heavyMaintenanceLease.staleAfterMs (config.template.mjs) = SSOT (env NEO_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS). - heavyMaintenanceLeasePrimitives.mjs: DEFAULT_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_TTL_MS removed; buildLeasePayload requires staleAfterMs + throws loudly without it. - All callers inject from the AiConfig-aware boundary: HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService (staleAfterMs_ reads the leaf) + MaintenanceBackpressureService + 7 entrypoint scripts (backfill/syncKB/syncGH/ingest/backup/runSandman/defrag) read the leaf at the use site. - Spec: test-wrappers default a valid TTL (suites test acquire/stale/inheritance, not the TTL value) + a guard test pins the required-staleAfterMs contract.

73 lease specs pass; node --check + block-alignment clean (10 files). The import-block whitespace re-aligns are the check-block-alignment --fix over-reach (#14212); git diff -w shows only the real changes. Resolves #14204.

* test(ai): align lease expectations with AiConfig (#14204)

* docs(ai): point lease TTL docs at AiConfig (#14204)
